steps.body: Hi Dana, I'm on a mission to scale HIPAA compliance software, and Cascade is one I really admire, the focus on clinics and small hospital systems especially. Would love to connect.

steps.subject: An admirer of what you've built at Cascade Compliance Systems
steps.body:
Hi Dana,

As I've been getting to know the HIPAA compliance world, Cascade Compliance Systems kept coming up, and what stuck with me is that you built for clinics and small hospital systems, the places that carry the most HIPAA risk but can least afford a full compliance department. Making real protection reachable for a ten-person clinic is harder, and more useful, than most people give it credit for.

I'm a former public defender, and part of that work was coordinating client health care services (chronic illness, mental health, etc.). I later spent time as Chief of Staff at a lab testing company, where HIPAA wasn't a box to check, it was something we lived with every day. Today I have a "search fund" backed by a dozen current and former CEOs, and it is my dream to scale HIPAA compliance software, done the right way, across the country.

I'd love to learn how you've built Cascade Compliance Systems, and if you ever think about what growth or the right partner could look like, I'd be glad to talk. Would you be open to a quick 20-minute call in the coming weeks?

Warmly,
Nico Goldberg

steps.subject: Re: An admirer of what you've built at Cascade Compliance Systems
steps.body:
Hi Dana,

Following up on my note below. I know a cold email about a partnership is easy to set aside, so no pressure to reply quickly.

If it is ever useful, I am glad to share what I have been learning about where HIPAA compliance is heading, even if Cascade Compliance Systems is not something you would ever consider changing. Mostly I am trying to understand the space from the people who actually built in it.

Warmly,
Nico

steps.subject: Connecting with a HIPAA compliance entrepreneur
steps.body:
Hi Dana,

I wanted to say a bit more about what I am actually after, since cold notes can come across the wrong way. I am not a fund looking to buy a company and strip it for parts. I would want to partner with a founder like you and help Cascade Compliance Systems grow into its next chapter without losing what makes it work in the first place.

If that is ever a conversation worth having, even a year or two from now, I would love twenty minutes. And if you would rather just trade notes on the field, I am glad to do that too.

Warmly,
Nico

steps.subject: Re: Connecting with a HIPAA compliance entrepreneur
steps.body:
Hi Dana,

I know you are busy, so just a short nudge. I am still very interested in Cascade Compliance Systems and happy to talk whenever the timing is right for you.

Warmly,
Nico

steps.subject: Comparing notes on where HIPAA compliance is headed
steps.body:
Hi Dana,

No agenda here, just something I keep turning over that you would actually know the answer to. As more clinics move to cloud EHRs, it looks from the outside like the hard part of HIPAA compliance is shifting from locking down the servers to tracking every vendor who touches the data. I may have that wrong, and I would value your read.

Either way, thank you for the work Cascade Compliance Systems does for those clinics.

Warmly,
Nico

steps.subject: Young entrepreneur still hoping to connect on Cascade Compliance Systems
steps.body:
Hi Dana,

This is my last note, so I will keep it short. I have a lot of respect for what you have built at Cascade Compliance Systems, and I did not want to keep filling your inbox.

If you ever want to trade notes on the field, or think about what a partnership could look like down the road, my door is open. Thank you for the work you do.

Warmly,
Nico
Hi Carl, I'm on a mission to scale commercial HVAC service companies, and Summit is one I really admire, the focus on keeping schools and offices running especially. Would love to connect.

steps.subject: An admirer of what you've built at Summit Mechanical Services
steps.body:
Hi Carl,

As I've been getting to know the commercial HVAC world, Summit Mechanical Services kept coming up, and what stuck with me is that you built a service business people actually rely on, the kind where a school or office calls and someone shows up and fixes it right. Keeping crews that good together, on contracts customers renew year after year, is harder than most people give it credit for.

I started out as a public defender, and a lot of that job was just showing up and doing the unglamorous work that kept things from falling apart for people. I later spent time as Chief of Staff at a growing company, where I learned how much a business lives or dies on its operations and its people. Today I have a "search fund" backed by a dozen current and former CEOs, and it is my dream to take one great commercial HVAC company and scale it the right way across the country.

I'd love to learn how you've built Summit Mechanical Services, and if you ever think about what growth or the right partner could look like, I'd be glad to talk. Would you be open to a quick 20-minute call in the coming weeks?

Warmly,
Nico Goldberg

Warmly,

steps.subject: Connecting with a commercial HVAC entrepreneur
steps.body:
Hi Carl,

I wanted to say a bit more about what I am actually after, since cold notes can come across the wrong way. I am not a fund looking to buy a company and strip it for parts. I would want to partner with an owner like you and help Summit Mechanical Services grow into its next chapter without losing the crew and the reputation that make it work.

If that is ever a conversation worth having, even a year or two from now, I would love twenty minutes. And if you would rather just trade notes on the trade, I am glad to do that too.

Warmly,
Nico

steps.subject: Re: Connecting with a commercial HVAC entrepreneur
steps.body:
Hi Carl,

I know you are busy, and your season does not slow down, so just a short nudge. I am still very interested in Summit Mechanical Services and happy to talk whenever the timing is right for you.

Warmly,
Nico

steps.subject: Comparing notes on where commercial HVAC is headed
steps.body:
Hi Carl,

No agenda here, just something I keep turning over that you would actually know. From the outside it looks like the shops that thrive are the ones that turned service and maintenance contracts into the backbone of the business, instead of living job to job on installs. I may have that wrong, and I would value your read.

Either way, thank you for the work Summit Mechanical Services does keeping those buildings running.

Warmly,
Nico

steps.body:
Hi Carl,

This is my last note, so I will keep it short. I have a lot of respect for what you have built at Summit Mechanical Services, and I did not want to keep filling your inbox.

If you ever want to trade notes on the trade, or think about what a partnership could look like down the road, my door is open. Thank you for the work you do.

Warmly,

Hi Marcus, I'm on a mission to scale legal case management software, and Lakeside is one I really admire, the small-firm focus especially. Would love to connect.

steps.subject: An admirer of what you've built at Lakeside Legal Software
steps.body:
Hi Marcus,

As I've been getting to know the legal case management world, Lakeside Legal Software kept coming up, and what stuck with me is that you built for small law firms, the two- and three-lawyer shops that run on whatever actually fits how they work, instead of chasing the big firms everyone else fights over. Building for the firms that need the help most, and have the least patience for clunky tools, is harder to get right than it looks.

I started out as a Client Advocate in the Alameda County Public Defender's Office, working in jails, clinics, and encampments to help people get through the legal system in one piece. That work taught me how much a case can turn on whether the people handling it can find the right document and hit the right date. Today I have a "search fund" backed by a dozen current and former CEOs, and it is my dream to scale legal case management software, built around how lawyers actually work, across the country.

I'd love to learn how you've built Lakeside Legal Software, and if you ever think about what growth or the right partner could look like, I'd be glad to talk. Would you be open to a quick 20-minute call in the coming weeks?

Warmly,
Nico Goldberg
